<!--
AUTHOR & AI GUIDANCE — read before writing this description.

This description is for HUMAN REVIEWERS. Write it so a reviewer can decide what
to look at first and why the change is safe.

- Git history keeps only the bulleted commit messages, NOT this description.
  So this description is the place for context, screenshots, and reasoning —
  keep the bullets here as a SUMMARY of what is most important to review, not an
  exhaustive changelog (the commits already cover that).
- Do NOT state that automated tests pass — CI already verifies that, and saying
  so adds noise. Only describe MANUAL testing you actually performed (steps,
  devices, what you observed).
- Include screenshots / recordings for any user-visible (UI) change. This app
  targets a 1404 x 1872 greyscale e-ink screen — show the relevant state.
- Delete any section below that does not apply. Keep it short and scannable.
-->

## Purpose

<!-- Why this change exists. The problem it solves or capability it adds.
     One or two sentences is usually enough. -->

## What to review (summary)

<!-- The most important points for a reviewer, as bullets. Lead with anything
     risky, subtle, or that you want a second opinion on. -->

-
-

## Screenshots / recordings

<!-- Required for user-visible changes. Before / after where helpful.
     Remove this section for non-visual changes. -->

| Before | After |
| ------ | ----- |
|        |       |

## Alternatives considered

<!-- Other approaches you weighed and why you didn't take them. Helps reviewers
     understand the design and avoid re-litigating settled choices. -->

-

## Manual testing

<!-- Only MANUAL verification you performed. Do NOT mention automated tests —
     CI covers those. List concrete steps and what you observed. -->

-

## Notes for reviewers

<!-- Anything else: follow-ups, known limitations, related issues, migration or
     data-model impact. Remove if empty. -->
